title: N-Hydroxy-N-p-Chlorophenyl-N'-(2,5-Dirnethyl) Phenyl-p-Toluarnidine Hydrochloride as an Analytical Reagent for Gravimetric Determination of Copper
author: K. S. PATEL
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, Ravishankar University, Raipnr-492 001 

  Manuscript received 15 February 1978, accepted 10 April 1978  

 N-Hydroxy-N- p -Chlorophenyi-N' -(2, 5-dimethyl) phenyl- p -toluamidine hydrochloride is proposed as a new sensitive and highly selective reagent for the gravimetric determination of copper(II). The thermally stable and directly weighable buff coloured copper complex of the reagent having the composition Cu(C 22 H 22 N 2 OCI). gets quantitatively precipitated in the  p H ran~e 3.7-11.0. Many foreign ions including Fe 3+  Co 2+  Mn 2+  Ni 2+ , Cd 2+ ,  \(MoO_4^{2+}\)  and  \(Wo_4^{2+}\)  can coexist during the determination with out any adverse effect. The infrared spectral studies of the reagent and the copper complex has been made, The method has been applied to various copper alloys.
